CATIA V5 ANSYS FEA SolidWorks OpenCV ISO 13485 Wireless
// Engineering Portfolio · Mechanical Designs · Robotics

Projects &
Mechanical Designs

"Good design is like a refrigerator — when it works, no one notices, but when it doesn't, it sure stinks." — Irene Au

Medical Robotics ISO 13485 MIS

Cancer Detection Tool for Minimal Invasive Surgery

A bespoke cancer detection tool designed for use in Minimal Invasive Surgery (MIS). The tool measures tissue stiffness via tactile feedback to help surgeons identify potentially cancerous tissue in real time — reducing the need for traditional biopsy. Designed to ISO 13485 medical device standards with full hazard and risk analysis.

  • Tissue stiffness measurement via piezoresistive sensor
  • Designed to ISO 13485 medical device standards
  • Hazard and Risk Analysis (FMEA)
  • Compatible with standard laparoscopic ports (5–12mm)
Tele-operation Wireless Systems Medical Devices

Tele-operated Robot for Medical Injection System

A tele-operated robotic platform designed for administering medical injections remotely, targeting emergency scenarios for wounded soldiers or patients in hazardous zones. The system uses low-latency wireless communication and an intuitive operator interface for precise drug delivery without physical proximity.

  • Initial Design & Prototype — mechanical frame and joint layout
  • Actuator integration and control system wiring
  • Isometric, Bottom & Top view assemblies
  • Sterile drug cartridge delivery sub-assembly
Mechanical Design FEA / ANSYS CATIA V5

High-Precision Gearbox Design

High-precision gearbox engineered for robotic joint actuation — from initial conceptualization through full CAD modelling, gear ratio calculations, tolerance analysis, and Finite Element Analysis (FEA). Optimized for load distribution and minimal backlash in robotic motion control applications.

  • Gear ratio: 8:1 reduction for robotic joint control
  • FEA simulation in ANSYS for stress concentration
  • Full BOM and manufacturing drawings in CATIA V5
  • Backlash tolerance < 0.05°
Product Design Contextual Design Human Factors

Sensor Mounting System & Product Design

A sensor mounting system designed for environmental monitoring platforms (linked to the ORiV project at IVY TECH). Applied a structured product design process across four core pillars.

  • Contextualisation of Knowledge
  • Praxis & Technique
  • Critical Thinking
  • Research & Communication
